posture: Watch (ran clean) — no fresh acute trigger surfaced across the free source register in the 24 hours to Friday morning, extending the quiet run to an eighth straight day and closing out the week. No new regulatory, delivery, social-licence, market, supply-chain, safety, political or cyber signal cleared the NSW-REZ relevance bar today. Standing exposures are unchanged and carried forward: (1) the New England REZ Access Scheme draft declaration is still not confirmed as released — EnergyCo's access-scheme page continues to show only the March-2026 revised timeline anticipating an end-June 2026 draft ahead of the end-September ministerial declaration, now roughly a fortnight past that target; (2) EnergyCo's New England corridor field-investigation program runs July-September across contested landholdings (150 affected landholders between Muswellbrook and Walcha along the refined 1km study corridor to Bayswater) while the Walcha High Country Guardians opposition and the Coalition's late-June energy policy (a New England REZ scope/route review that would scrap the 500kV corridor, plus proposed urban and Outback REZs) stay live into the March 2027 state-election run-up; and (3) on Central-West Orana, ACEREZ's late-June position that it has recommenced access-track work under stronger controls (internal rock-shelter findings submitted to DPHI) remains the latest word, with the departmental assessment of the heritage breach still the open thread. Logistics milestone still worth tracking: oversized turbine-blade deliveries to Uungula via the Port-to-REZ route are flagged to begin around July 2026 along the Newcastle-to-Central-West-Orana corridor.
summary: A full scan of the free source register returned no new NSW-REZ-relevant development in the 24 hours to this morning. Every thread that surfaced — the New England access-scheme timing, the July-September field-investigation program, the Walcha-area and Coalition opposition, the ACEREZ Central-West Orana rock-shelter matter, the flagged start of oversized turbine-blade movements to Uungula, and routine South West REZ project-consultation deadlines (e.g. the Sundown Solar Farm submission window that closed 8 July) — is either a prior-digest entry with no fresh movement or dated/procedural background outside the 24-hour window, and is deduplicated out per the writing rules. Today is published with empty sections as a continuity / 'ran clean' marker. The most material standing signal remains procedural: EnergyCo has still not confirmed public release of the New England REZ Access Scheme draft declaration against its end-June target, keeping a low-grade schedule-slippage watch live ahead of the end-September ministerial declaration and the March 2027 state-election backdrop.
